title: World's Best Rodeo (and World's Best Verizon Store)
summary:
After leaving Yellowstone, we headed to Cody to run the life errands. We got groceries, filled up water, I worked for a couple of hours and we luckily got hooked up with a chiller at the Verizon store who was able to finagle a new and basically free phone for Colin. After taking Maya on a little walk outside of town, I spent the afternoon laying in the city park while Colin went on a little run. We had a big cobb salad in the park before heading over to the rodeo. One of my goals while in Wyoming was to go to a rodeo and Cody did not dissapoint. They have a rodeo every single night in the summer, complete with bull riding, bareback bronco riding, roping, kids chasing chows, barrel racing and silly audience participation. Although we have both lived in Montana for a few years and seen some of the ranch life, this was definitely somewhat of a cultural experience, with much more patriotic musings and prayers than we are used to. My favorite part was this 8 year old superstar cowgirl who was incredible to watch and my least favorite part was watching a horse demolish a rider who had to be taken out in an ambulance. Ending at almost 10pm, we drove a little ways out of town to some BLM land. We haven't had many late night arrivals but it's kind of fun to wake up in the morning to a beautiful view that we didn't know was there the night before.

-Helen

photos.caption: Helen coaxing Maya into the waters of the Shoshone River
